secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with fintech/structured investments domain focus

level_signals: Minimum of 2 years of experience in product management
Base salary range $100K-$130K consistent with associate-level PM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum of 2 years of experience in product management, preferably within fintech, wealth management, or related industries.

A willingness and initiative to become an expert in the structured investments space, understanding both the product intricacies and wider industry to drive informed decision making and product leadership.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in a results-driven, entrepreneurial environment.
Strong analytical skills, with a proven track record of solving complex, unfamiliar problems through innovative solutions.
Experience with agile methodologies and a mindset that embraces quick deployment, learning, and iteration.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to develop clear and effective project communication plans.
A customer-centric approach, with experience engaging directly with clients to gather insights and feedback.
Technical acumen, comfortable working closely with engineering teams to solve problems and complete projects.
Detail-oriented, with the ability to write comprehensive user stories and acceptance criteria.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Preferred experience with JIRA, Confluence, Figma, analytics dashboards, or Postman.

scope.business_model: B2B fintech platform serving wealth management firms, financial advisors, structured investments issuers, and wholesalers
primary_metrics_inferred: Product adoption and usage by advisors/issuers
core_responsibilities: Engage with wealth management firms, financial advisors, structured investments issuers, and wholesalers to identify gaps in the market and propose innovative solutions
Define product requirements by writing detailed user stories and acceptance criteria for software engineers
Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of product feature development from conception to launch, including testing and iteration
Own the product roadmap for specific features or segments of the platform
Communicate effectively with business stakeholders to gather requirements, provide updates, and align product vision and strategy
Develop and maintain comprehensive project communication plans
Investigate bugs and identify tactical solutions to production issues
Monitor the performance of product features post-launch and continuously seek opportunities for improvement

compensation.equity_form: equity for all full-time employees (form not specified)

green_flags: Equity offered to all full-time employees
Comprehensive benefits including 100% employer-paid dental, vision, telemedicine, and virtual mental health counseling
Clear product ownership and end-to-end responsibility described
Explicitly growth-oriented role with room to expand responsibilities
red_flags: No city explicitly named in the JD body for this role — office location unclear without additional context
Hybrid policy is Mon-Thu in office with only Friday remote — nearly full in-person commitment

ambiguities: The preferred experience with tools (JIRA, Confluence, Figma, analytics dashboards, Postman) is listed in the same paragraph as hard requirements under 'Candidate Profile'; classified as nice-to-have based on the word 'Preferred'.

The minimum 2 years PM experience qualifier 'preferably within fintech, wealth management, or related industries' could be read as either a hard requirement or a soft preference — treated as hard for the years minimum, with industry preference noted in the field.
